subject: Ecology
title: Reef Symbioses
description: A quick, practical guide to Reef Symbioses.

hook.text: Clownfish nestle safely in anemone tentacles that would kill almost any other fish.

buildup.text: The clownfish is coated in mucus that prevents the sting. In return, it guards the anemone.

discovery.text: Cleaner wrasses eat parasites off larger fish, who line up at 'cleaning stations' to wait.

twist.text: Some shrimp share burrows with gobies—the shrimp digs, the goby watches for predators.

climax.text: Reefs run on partnerships. Nearly every resident depends on at least one mutualism.

punchline.text: On a reef, cooperation is the rent.

quiz.question: What do cleaner wrasses do for larger fish?
